description: This figure presents a bright-field (BF) TEM image and the corresponding energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) elemental mapping for sample M2-30, a Ti/V-modified Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel aged at 550 °C for 30 h. The BF micrograph identifies a (Ti,V)C carbide particle and an adjacent κ-carbide precipitate at its edge. The eight elemental distribution maps for Fe, Mn, Al, Si, Cr, Mo, V, and Ti reveal strong V and Ti enrichment at the (Ti,V)C carbide, while Fe represents the austenite matrix. Mn, Al, Si, Cr, and Mo show relatively uniform distributions without pronounced segregation to the precipitates. All panels share a 50 nm scale bar. These observations provide direct microchemical evidence supporting the conclusion that pre-existing (Ti,V)C carbides act as heterogeneous nucleation sites for κ-carbide precipitation, thereby modifying the precipitation morphology compared to the uniform intragranular κ-carbide distribution in Ti/V-free variants.
